response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2000–2001 and a bachelor's thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of professional C++ on Windows. He has no demonstrable fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain expertise — the one role with 'Capital' in the name (RIDE Capital) was a fintech startup using Node and TypeScript. SQL Server and Oracle are not mentioned, and his entire career trajectory points toward web, mobile, and developer-tooling work rather than financial systems development.
strengths: Extensive overall career demonstrating the ability to learn and apply diverse languages and paradigms across many domains
Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps experience (GitLab, Docker, CI servers) across multiple roles
Strong communication skills evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and open-source project leadership
concerns: C++ professional experience is negligible (~6 months in 2000–2001), nowhere near the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
Zero fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge anywhere in the resume
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle usage; Windows platform development is entirely absent from the career history
